_comment: Offline jobs run by `npm run offline:all` (scripts/offline/run-all.ts). Add/remove/toggle freely — each runs in order even if a prior one fails. Findings land in reckons-workspace/knowledge.pending.jsonl for review in Reckons.AI. cmd runs in bash, so ${VAR:-default} and pipes work.
_tiers: F74.3 work tiering. tier=script: deterministic, no LLM, zero tokens, right by construction — run these first and prefer growing this tier. tier=agent: a LOCAL model fills a judgment hole inside a scripted harness (ground → prompt → validate → emit-proposal) and only ever emits PROPOSALS a human gates. Opus is not a tier here: it designs the harness and triages the queue these jobs fill. Anything an agent job gets right reliably should be demoted to a script.
_blocking: blocking=true means `run-all --ci` exits non-zero if this job fails. Reserve it for failures you cannot take back after a push. Everything else is ADVISORY: it reports and queues pending findings, and never blocks a merge — a gate that cries wolf gets switched off, and then it protects nothing.

jobs.name: published-graph-guard
jobs.desc: static/knowledge.ttl IS the public graph (served at /knowledge.ttl). Checks it parses, is not suddenly empty, and holds no test debris. Was found gutted 1032 -> 45 statements, one `git add .` from publishing test junk to the world

jobs.name: build-guard
jobs.desc: Is ./build actually a PRODUCTION build? No dev artifacts (@vite/client), minified, content-hashed, CSP present. Disabled here because it needs a build to exist — it runs in CI right after `npm run build` (npm run build:verify). Enable with --only=build-guard after building locally

jobs.name: claim-audit
jobs.desc: ASYNC alignment: sweeps the hand-written surfaces align(1) cannot generate (README, SAFETY.md, COUNSEL-BRIEF.md, app shell, landing copy) for claims the graph contradicts — an unbuilt feature described as working, or a countable claim that is simply wrong. Emits PROPOSALS for review; never gates a build, because 'is this sentence overclaiming' is a judgment

jobs.name: history-lessons
jobs.desc: Mines this repo's OWN history for how it fails: fixes that shipped with no test (a bug with a return ticket — in a semantic system the regression will not crash, it will quietly go back to being wrong) and fragility hotspots. Evidence, not opinion

jobs.name: graph-lint
jobs.desc: Graph invariants: dead has-file/tested-by links, bad status, duplicate feature-ids, dangling refs, incomplete features

jobs.name: status-evidence
jobs.desc: Shipped features must link a test OR declare kpred:test-coverage "none" — you may ship untested code, but not silently

jobs.name: shared-deps
jobs.desc: Whole-roadmap shared-dependency scan — dependency hubs (build once), shared files, and overlap warnings (features that need the same thing but touch no common file). Avoid separate code for nearly the same task.

jobs.name: code-sprawl
jobs.desc: Copy-paste / duplication detector — the same block of code in >1 file (the disease behind the clipboard crash and the label-overlay drift), plus file-size sprawl. The code-level companion to shared-deps.

jobs.name: safety-attestation
jobs.desc: Verify the good-faith safety controls still hold (preamble present/injected/undrifted, classifier, safety tests, egress gating). Add --record to append a dated entry to static/reckons-safety-log.ttl

jobs.name: prompt-audit
jobs.desc: System-prompt token cost, ETHICS_PREAMBLE share per prompt, preamble drift between app and mcp-server, ungated prompts

jobs.name: alignment-sweep
jobs.desc: TTL parse / asset paths / namespace typos / stale LEAP + branch-align --suggest + kb-align → pending questions

jobs.name: competitor-scan
jobs.desc: F86: refresh competitor metadata + LICENSE from the GitHub API; flags license changes, copyleft (we are MIT), unlicensed repos, and abandoned projects. Add --discover to search for new competitors. Never edits the TTL — judgment stays human

jobs.name: button-crawl
jobs.desc: F34: clicks every interactive element on every route; flags crashes, blank screens, SILENT no-ops, and sub-44px touch targets. Pure local JS (no Ollama). Disabled by default because it needs a server already running — `npm run dev`, then enable this or run `npm run offline:all -- --only=button-crawl`

jobs.name: graph-economics
jobs.desc: F74.5: both sides of the token ledger — TTL maintenance churn and MCP tool-schema tax (debit) against compressed-context saving, cited avoided-rework, and the extend-vs-add cohesion ratio (credit) — plus break-even and the compounding curve. Advisory, never blocking: a ledger reports, it does not gate. Reports correlation and cited events only; there is no repo built without a graph to compare against

jobs.name: describe-entities
jobs.desc: Local model drafts kpred:description for entities graph-lint found incomplete, grounded in their own subgraph + linked files (needs OLLAMA_BASE_URL)

jobs.name: code-review
jobs.desc: Local coding model (qwen3-coder) reviews the branch diff → findings to pending review (needs OLLAMA_BASE_URL)

jobs.name: visual-diff
jobs.desc: Local-VLM visual regression prod↔dev (needs OLLAMA_BASE_URL; set VISUAL_DIFF_BASE / VISUAL_DIFF_HEAD)
